title: Adding an UNDO feature in Activity tab
excerpt: Adding an undo feature in the Activity tab to reduce the need to manually reenter information that may accidentally get cleared from cells.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding an undo feature in the Activity tab to reduce the need to manually reenter information that may accidentally get cleared from cells.

posts.author: katcamb (Katie Whitaker)
posts.cookedHtml: I would be interested in this as well. I would suggest an undo button for the entire board, so if you change information, move a pulse, re-name a group, etc. It would undo the last item if you click on it once, and then if 2x for the next item back, and so on. Even if only one undo back is possible, that would help out immensely!

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
@kyrinfield and @katcamb

This is a great suggestion! I’d like to run with your suggestions and take this even further.
Imagine having a (Restore) at the activity level for each change. (Probably want this to be only available to BOARD Owners) Something like this.

See screenshot below. Look at the little Undo icons inside the orange parenthesis.

posts.author: katcamb (Katie Whitaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
@alejenciam
I love this suggestion and would like to see it added!

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml:
I totally agree. Ideally ctrl+z would be great, but I understand the complications of this in a shared environment. I think adding a simple undo or restore feature in the activity tab is the very least that could be done.

I know a we have already had a handful of accidents of team members clicking the “x” next to the cell instead of clicking into it. They then have to manually input the information by clicking between the board view and the activity view to reenter the information.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
we have already had a handful of accidents of team members clicking the “x” next to the cell instead of clicking into it.

Good to hear others have similar issues to us.

No Undo can make it painful to get back to where you were.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml: I totally agree. Seems like major missing feature.

posts.author: Majormind (Tobias Brücker)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is needed, also in our project!

posts.author: Adrian (Adrian F. Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
@alejencia
I love that suggestion!

posts.author: Majormind (Tobias Brücker)
posts.cookedHtml: I just had an idea: you could add the “undo-button” in the activity log/sidebar

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t understand how this isn’t already a feature. Undo is a pretty basic function that is much needed.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone, how about that Undo button, am I right?
We just had another issue over the holidays that cause hours of work to recover information that was lost that could have EASILY been solved with by simply clicking Undo.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump. PLEASE up vote this.
Had another issue within our team regarding this feature.

posts.author: scoobysnack (Christina McGuire)
posts.cookedHtml: We are just evaluating Monday and as the PM I’m a little worried about this as it’s a big ask getting people to move from Basecamp to Monday and the possible ease of things being deleted is not good. I though I read somewhere there is an Undo, but I can’t find it anywhere… I could see people deleting a column in a group not realising it will disappear in all groups.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is an undo for deleting full columns, but to my knowledge it only shows a small notification at the top of the board that basically gives you 5-10 seconds to undo.

However, if you accidentally delete a cell with information in it (which is fairly easy since when you hover over a cell a small “x” comes up that will delete the whole cell), there is no way to get that back. We have an inventory board and a number of our team members have accidentally deleted serial numbers and tracking numbers from cells.

This feature needs to be made a priority with their feature/dev team.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
kyrinfield:

However, if you accidentally delete a cell with information in it (which is fairly easy since when you hover over a cell a small “x” comes up that will delete the whole cell), there is no way to get that back.

Agreed. One of our main peeves with Monday.com.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, it’s a bummer that this issue isn’t getting enough attention to climb the list of development tasks for their dev team.

posts.author: Majormind (Tobias Brücker)
posts.cookedHtml: We have this issue as well on the top of our wishlist. Since many things are logged anyway in the “history”, it makes no sense to limit the undo-possibilities technically.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All - thank you for your ongoing feedback on this matter, I totally understand the need for this.

We have started to look into these functionalities, as we now have a granted feature for the undoing of items that are moved from one board to another.

Would love to hear from you on how you’d hope for this to work – are you looking to have an undo option for all actions within the activity log? Which one’s are the most important?

Thanks as always for contributing and helping to make our platform

posts.author: Majormind (Tobias Brücker)
posts.cookedHtml: Dear Brett! For us, we’d like to have an “undo”-button for all actions (as in photoshop with a step-protocol, to choose exactly, how many steps back we want to go). Important is, that the last three steps could be undone. Thanks for your work!

posts.author: nikolamujdzic (Nikola Mujdzic Rescic)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Brett,

recently I have created a board for application to NGO. Created a form and then duplicated the board. Board 1 had active form view and received entered data from the applicants through the form. So far I have had more than 10.000 members and that means 10 K pulses. In board 1 i have 10 people verifying VAT numbers etc, and giving statuses verified, wrong data etc…

After verification I moved pulses to Board 2, as that board was members board. That means that they were verified NGO members. Often happened that during the batch actions, selection of the 100-500 pulses remain after batch status change, and someone pasted corrected VAT number into 300 pulses instead of only intended one. It would be great to have batch undo action in activity log.

posts.author: deathbychocolate (Daniel Canestaro-Garcia)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re also pining for this feature.
We’re doing a lot more on Monday and losing information we input is making us reconsider our subscription.
I really like alejencia comment from October of 2019 with the mockup. Being able to pop up a menu of changes to roll back and select one, or shift select multiple might (?) be easier to implement on a shared board than just a keyboard shortcut. If could apply to the entire board rather than a row. But either way would be helpful. 10 second popup is just enough time to review and register that you need to hit it before your mouse runs over to juuuuust miss the button. A short term fix would be expanding that to 30 seconds. But a longer term ability to roll back changes is really necessary.

posts.author: beacorona (Beatriz)
posts.cookedHtml: I just lost 3 days work with a status change that I can’t undo. Although the 10 second UNDO button is useful. It is not enough. I didn’t notice that the changes had applied to other groups as well. This seems very basic and after spending so much time configuring this tool I am thinking of going back to GDocs. If I made that mistake, it will be something that can be easily done by others who are less tech savvy which will cause hours of work to recover data (if at all possible). VERY frustrating.

posts.author: editarr (Ed.Arr)
posts.cookedHtml:
An undo feature would be very crucial for text changes as well, especially since those changes don’t even have a count-down timer to reverse them immediately.
When I am working on a board with a lot of text and accidentally click the “x” in the corner of a text box (which when the columns are not very big happens quite easily), there is nothing I can do to get the text back except look at the activity log and re-type everything manually.
Very annoying. It would already be a big help if the text in the activity log could be copied, but an un-do feature is long overdue as well.

posts.author: caro (Carolina Mobarac)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any updates on this? It is indeed very crucial!

posts.author: kerstyn (Kerstyn)
posts.cookedHtml: We could do with this. We’ve had a problem recently of files being accidentally deleted from the file column. There is no way to get them back.

posts.author: LeahJ (Leah Jaques)
posts.cookedHtml: The 10 second “undo” is not enough! And it doesn’t cover changes to column/board settings. It would be great to go into the activity log and restore previous versions of the board like you can on Google Docs. Hope you will implement this feature soon!

posts.author: Randi1 (Randi Fuchs)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m so shocked that undo with control+z isn’t possible yet (after over 1 year of this feedback). What’s strange is that when I hit control+z, it activates the “Edit” tab in my menu (on Mac) but it doesn’t actually do anything…

A restore version feature would also be great! I thought it was available when I first saw the activity thread but wasn’t clickable (which makes that pretty useless).

posts.author: goldmandw (Dylan)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to see an undo feature too. It seems like a pretty basic feature that has been overlooked.

posts.author: CMathys (Cynthia Mathys)
posts.cookedHtml: Undo is really needed to reduce the impact of accidentally clicking on the “x” inside a cell when it’s difficult or time-consuming to recover the data lost. Having a reliable undo function would also bolster the case (at least in my org) that switching to monday.com doesn’t introduce any significant risk of data loss or user error.

posts.author: Heatherbiene (Heather Bienefelt)
posts.cookedHtml: having the deleted text visible in the activity log is one thing but it’s not able to be clicked or copied so you have to manually enter it. The undo in the activity log would solve SO many problems

posts.author: Altuijri (Ahmad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Today by mistake, I selected 39 items, and tried to change one item (the whole row) and after I finished, I found out I changes over 500 cell. Imagine, I have to go manually and change them one by one!!!
AHHHH How much I hate this

posts.author: mikepans1013 (Michael Pansolini)
posts.cookedHtml: Hopping on board this thread to also request an undo feature for our organization

posts.author: mondayuser123 (Kevin Hoffman)
posts.cookedHtml: I will no longer be recommending Monday to friends and colleagues because of the lack of this feature. An accidental edit of several hundred items has cost my organization days of work. Monday support has been non-existent after 2 days of requesting help. I don’t see how something so simple is not built-in from the start.

posts.author: mingoes (mingoes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Came here especially to vote on this issue as well. Just going through the activity log and manually set back items is a painful process.
Made us wary of integrating more of our business with Monday.

posts.author: NigelMarsh (Nigel Marsh)
posts.cookedHtml: The lack of undo is an unwelcome surprise. We’re part way through a 3 month test out of Monday pro with a view to moving to Enterprise. All was looking good until the discovery that whole blocks of work can disappear at the click of a misapplied button - with no recourse. We are told by Monday that they ‘might add it as a feature in the future’. Clearly not a top priority.

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml: The good is that you can view a sort of’an audit on everything changes in the activity tab. The bad is that if you wanted to revert, you would have to do it manually. I have a feeling that when this feature gets around to be in production … it will be only for Enterprise, that just my guess based on other things that have happened with features. Best of luck @NigelMarsh

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is one of those things that sounds simple, but isn’t exactly straightforward.

  1. While almost everything that happens on an item is listed in the Activity Logs, not everything is.
  2. Because actions on an item can trigger any number of Automations and Integrations, an Undo feature can very quickly create data that is way out of sync with the way workflows have been designed.
  3. If you are monday․com or an app developer, do you design the feature to also reverse back any Automations or Integrations that triggered? What if something else happened in the meantimes since the “bad” change - should that be reversed too? What ramifications might that have on other Automations that may now trigger inadvertently from reversing previous actions?

It quickly turns into a programming quagmire. That’s why we emphasize in all of our monday․com training session to use bulk edit with care and watch for that friendly green Undo button!

posts.author: patricia.ousley (Patricia Ousley)
posts.cookedHtml: I just accidentally changed all the text for an ENTIRE BOARD and lost all my work. This feature is basic, if this doesn’t get fixed soon I will be considering other platforms.

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: Just adding my voice here. I’m fairly new to Monday, commited to a full year, and I really can’t believe there’s no undo.

posts.author: Phil-Sheppard (Phil Sheppard)
posts.cookedHtml: The Help article on Activity Logs says that when an item in the Log is clicked, an option in the resulting window is to undo the action. When I click on an item (on which I executed an action, not a colleague), the resulting window just says who created the board. No undo or any other option is given. Has anyone else had this problem?

posts.author: uly (Uly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, is there a way to undo changes on Activity logs view?
As of now, I think the only option to undo is the popup shown by Monday when you just made a modification on an item, once you ignored the popup there’s no other way to do the undo but to manually check the activity log.

It would be good if under activity log, an option is available to undo specific 1 or more actions at once.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 this would be great! Especially b/c looking through the activity log is when you’re most likely to see a mistake. Currently you would have to manually set the field back to the previous value.

posts.author: KristinLeefromLHL (Kristin)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was looking for a feature where you could undo more than once or if there was something like a version history where would could go back to a previous version. There’s the “Undo” pop up as well as the change log but it cannot undo more than one action.

Is this something that could be possible for a future implementation?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone, thank you for your request and suggestions. The development team had a look at this request and while they are considering making some improvements with the undo option, it’s difficult to say if it will affect the activity log. Learn more about what it means a request is marked as under consideration here

As part of our new feature request process, we are taking top request and discussing them with our product team. Read more about the new feature request process here.

posts.author: Rossella-WEHI (Rossella)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everybody

I have started using Monday.com three weeks ago (after having used Smartsheet for almost two years) and I was very very very surprised to say the least that the ‘Undo’ or ‘Restore’ capability in the Activity Log is not an option in Monday.com. It is paramount to have this enabled, as everybody (even the most expert Monday.com user can make mistakes.

It is part of the learning process to try things out before confirming changes, so I would request with urgency that this capability is integrated in the Monday.com features.

Thanks

posts.author: MelissaEPA (Melissa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bit strange that youve flagged a solution when there isnt a solution yet.

I have lost SO much time having to remember all the activity text I’ve accidentally deleted. Its near-criminal that undo isnt a thing given how much we pay for the service.

P.S. I dont know why Monday.com tick box radials are so VERY buggy. I seem to lose text most often when this is inside the activity box.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Melissa,

We added an update to let you know that this feature is now under consideration and our team is working on it. This is part of our new feature feedback process. Check out the new process and keep voting for your features since they do impact our development decisions.

All you need to know about our new Feature Request process.

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Everyone!

Exciting news ! LogBack is here! This new app, announced in monday marketplace, is a game-changer for board management.

With LogBack, you can effortlessly restore most of your board’s activity log, minimizing the risk of data loss and human mistakes.

We encourage you to give the app a try and recommend it to anyone you believe would find it relevant

Click here to add LogBack to your monday account

Frame1815×953 146 KB

posts.author: Avigail_Shay (Avigail)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, Seems like an efficient solution for undo actions.
Does it function on every board, including the exciting ones? @Dor_appSolution

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
Sure, the app works on any board.

posts.author: cat.goliath (Cat)
posts.cookedHtml: We just had an accident where someone removed hundreds of pieces of data from our board, and this was a life saver! Thank you!!

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m glad to hear that you found the app useful

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m not sure if this is some kind of joke. The most basic feature (UNDO) is “under consideration”? As in, you might decide “nah, not work the effort, if customers lose their data, so be it”?

I’m curious. Does anyone here use ANY app that has NO undo? I don’t think I have any.

posts.author: TanyaB (Tanya B)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am using large data in Monday.com. It looks like something can be undone, producing a green notification at the top to undo within a minute. However, if I change the content in a cell, I cannot undo this! It means if I make a mistake eg type the wrong text into the wrong row, I loose the original data forever!

I currently take regular exports of my board so I have a previous version but undo is a very basic ability that all programs have…

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Tanya,

Thanks for your feature request.

We hear you and recognise there is demand for this feature. Are you able to confirm if the existing feature request here, matches your request? This way we can merge the two, to consolidate votes and put greater emphasis on this need

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Everyone ,

You are welcome to try our latest LogBack version- now with capability to restore up to 25 board’s activities simultaneously!

New version for LogBack - Restore UP TO 25 board's activities at once monday apps & integrations
Hello We are so excited to share that LogBack released new version With this new version, you now have the capability to restore UP TO 25 board activities, since board creation, simultaneously every single time! Not only that, but we’ve also worked hard to improve overall performance, making sure everything runs even smoother. Don’t hesitate and click here to give it a try [Frame (1)]

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been testing the app, and it’s ok. The problem is that it relies in the (broken) activity log feature. I have been working for the past 30 minutes, updating dates and stuff like that. Nothing got recorded.

I then accidentally clicked on the “delete my cell data without warning or undo for no practical reason”. Information gone.

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Jorge, LogBack relies on the monday activity logs API. If you’re facing any problems with your account, such as actions not being recorded in the activity log, I suggest contacting monday for support.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community,

After hearing all your feedback and considering different ways to go about this feature, I am excited to share that adding more undo capabilities has been added to our roadmap. Our development team have already started developing the undo feature in the activity lab and are working on adding more undo capabilities - such as people and file columns.

Thank you for joining us in our development process and we are looking forward to hearing more great ideas from our community.

More about our feature request process here:

All you need to know about our new Feature Request process Feature requests
Welcome to the feature request part of our community! This is your opportunity to get involved in our development process. Share and vote on features you would like to see implemented in the platform so we can build a roadmap together that best reflects your needs. How do you submit a feature request? First, check if your idea already exists in the "feature request" category and vote on that topic. That way, our team can see which features are most requested. You can use the search function o…

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml:
While that’s good news, from the way you word this it seems you are still moving in the weird, non-standard Undo implementation of the few items that allow undo at the moment - i.e. the floating bubble that lets you undo a few choice things.

Every other app in the world has a standard undo feature, with a standard history, standard keystrokes, etc.

Anything you do in the app should have an undo, simple as that. If you can change data, you need to be able to change it back.

posts.author: greenersolutions (Kyla Kryski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here to say again how critical this is. It’s so easy to be in the item card view and accidentally select all items on the board behind that window and not realized that any chance you make is now made on every.single.item.

ANND now our team has to manually edit 342 items X 2 for the two fields that were accidentally bulk-changed.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
As many of you have seen, adding undo capabilities is expected to come soon! We know that his has been an important request for the community, so our product team is dedicated to making it happen. Stay tuned and make sure to turn on your notifications for this post to receive an update on the status. This feature was announced in our 2024 roadmap session at Elevate '23, thanks to our community feedback! In case you missed the session, you can watch the live 2024 roadmap session here. I know this has been a very important feature for many of us and want to thank you for all the thoughtful feedback. This year our product team is dedicated to trying to get as many request from our community into the roadmap as possible

To stay up informed of all updates on this post, make sure to turn on notifications for this topic by clicking on the bell on the the bottom right of this post.

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
Amber here from the product team. As Juliette mentioned, we have added this request to our roadmap and are hoping to release it in the second half of the year!
Looking forward to getting this feature out to you as soon as possible while making sure we maintain the best user experience

posts.author: sxf0 (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml: I am baffled that it has taken so long to implement this. The fact that it wasn’t a top priority for Q1 2024 is frustrating.

posts.author: ksur (Kimberli Sur)
posts.cookedHtml: Current keyboard shortcuts include Search, Copy, Paste and Cut. It would save so much time being able to ‘undo’ with Control/Command + Z.

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree x100 please make this a feature!

posts.author: Marie (Marie)
posts.cookedHtml: I agreed. this will be very useful

posts.author: Jonna (Jonnalyn Fitzpatrick)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes!!! Please allow the ability to undo errors made while updating data in the boards. I can’t count how many times I have had to re-enter data. The only undo option is right clicking within the browser which only takes you back 1 step.

posts.author: TIKTAL (Cyril)
posts.cookedHtml:
Simple: Being able to go back to any previous state with a CTRL+Z / CTRL+Shit+Z
Mainly used for items, groups, status, boards, data deletion, columns, groups, etc…

posts.author: Doughty94 (Darrin Doughty)
posts.cookedHtml:
I believe we all had the issue when we accidentally deleted the phone number, email or other important information from an item card. If we had a redo and undo button, this will be a life saver!

Way to implement it: Have the redo and undo button on the top of the board with the rest of the tools. As well as redo and undo buttons in the item card view.

posts.author: alexisstroman (alexis stroman)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! An undo function (like in Excel) is definitely needed.

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe there’s no undo. It’s driving me crazy! It also makes me not to trust the tool with any important data, since it’s so easy to wipe it out by accident.

posts.author: sarahklein (Sarah Klein)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m sure we have all accidentally deleted a phone number or changed an address on accident! As someone who uses monday.com as our CRM, we cannot accidentally delete this information! An undo/redo button is SO needed to avoid small mistakes like this!!

posts.author: czytomasz (tomasz pytel)
posts.cookedHtml:
The permanent Undo button to enable undo more than one action instead of current Undo popup lasting 30 seconds with undo only one action.
This would be the epic feature! I think customers could love this in Monday

posts.author: willjdry (Will)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately it is all too easy to accidently delete data from a board and it seems that such actions cannot be undone and the date cannot be recovered.

This has happened a few times to our team members and we can only restore the deleted data by copypasting it from other systems.

An undo last action button would be very useful to instantly correct such mistakes or having the ability to lock columns to stop data being deleted so easily.

posts.author: sarahklein (Sarah Klein)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m really surprised this doesn’t exist. I’m not a programmer/coder, but this seems like a simple feature to implement. We’ve had the same issue where something gets easily deleted and then we are either out of luck or have to hunt down the data elsewhere.

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, can we please have this be a feature? Very very concerned about how many basic features like this still aren’t yet implemented despite years of people asking for them. The deeper my org gets into Monday, the more I realized how unfinished the whole system is. @BiancaT

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml: Instead of a paid solution I would rather be able to CTRL-Z for free

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Timo, The lack of this feature in monday is precisely what the app aims to effectively address.
Anyway, CTRL-Z typically enables you to undo an action immediately after it’s performed rather than at a time of your choosing.

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: Well, the “x” icon that clears the value of a cell when you hover over it and accidentally click leaves nothing in the activity log. So there’s not even a manual undo for it. I’m baffled by this design choice.

posts.author: cynthiacorley7 (Cynthia Corley)
posts.cookedHtml: Can we address this please!!! We have accidentally deleted data in cells so many times because it is just too easy to click on the X icon to delete the data and there is no way to UNDO or retrieve the lost data in the activity log. This would be a game changer for us.

posts.author: rigwell (Jackson Philby)
posts.cookedHtml: How is this possible! this is absolutely ridiculous. Just lost a phone number that I accidently deleted with the “x” soooo stupid.

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @cynthiacorley7 @rigwell, You can use the LogBack, which allows the undo of a variety of actions on the board.
You can even undo actions that took place before you install the app.

Click here to try

posts.author: akeller (Aveleigh Keller)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t want to install a new app - I would like to have a simple “undo” action (control + Z) that is an instant undo. Not a popup that disappears after one minute. The app sounds great for larger items that you’ve deleted in the past, but if I accidentally delete a column, I shouldn’t have to log into a DIFFERENT app to simply undo the delete. Right?

posts.author: akeller (Aveleigh Keller)
posts.cookedHtml: Oh wow. Just tried the app - it doesn’t even do what you say it does. I can’t undo “column deleted.” Please - just stay simple. Control + Z please.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Everyone! Going to merge this with our existing thread about the topic where you will be able to see updates about the feature! Adding an UNDO feature in Activity tab

posts.author: TanyaB (Tanya B)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am using large data in Monday.com. It looks like something can be undone, producing a green notification at the top to undo within a minute. However, if I change the content in a cell, I cannot undo this! It means if I make a mistake eg type the wrong text into the wrong row, I loose the original data forever!

I currently take regular exports of my board so I have a previous version but undo is a very basic ability that all programs have…

posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone, our team is still this feature is still in the research and design phase. of adding the undo feature in the activity tab. It is planned to be released before the end of the year and will update as I know more about the release schedule!

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team . Thank you so much for your patience as we’ve worked toward an update on the undo feature in the activity tab, we know how highly requested this has been.

We’re excited to share that this is planned for release in Q3 2025! You’ll be able to undo actions on the table, including changing column values, deleting items, moving items, and batch actions for all of the above. This functionality will be accessible through the Activity Log.

We truly appreciate all of your thoughtful feedback, and it’s played a big part in shaping this functionality. We’ll be sure to keep you updated as we get closer to launch!

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @amybronstein appreciate this update on expectations for release of this feature. We see this as a critical piece of the path to true Enterprise functionality.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone! Shannon here with the monday team. I’m excited to share that undo in the Activity Log has been fully released! You can now access the undo button next to an activity within your Activity Log. The button will appear for the following actions:

  • Deleted items and board views

  • Column value changes

  • Items moved to another board

You can reverse the action by clicking the undo button, which will appear in the Activity Log for actions performed within the last 7 days. This includes Batch Action changes! With the correct board permissions, users can revert the actions completed by other users, and the undo action will appear as a new log in the Activity Log.

Check out this Knowledge Base article for more details: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360011810600-How-to-undo-something. Thank you all for your ongoing feedback and patience.

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there everyone! If you’re often checking board activity logs, Master Activity Logs may be helpful for your team. It acts as a ‘Master Activity Log’ for user activities across multiple boards. It offers advanced reporting based on that data as well. Hopefully that is helpful!
